title: Grace Lush
text:
Hello, welcome to my E-Portfolio.
My name is Grace Lush, a graduate of the University of Guelph and a current master’s student at the University of British Columbia. I completed my undergraduate degree in Environmental Science in 2025, where I developed a strong foundation in ecological systems, environmental analysis, and applied environmental management.
During my undergraduate studies, I completed several co-op positions in environmental-related roles, which provided valuable hands-on experience and helped bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world environmental work. These positions allowed me to gain practical skills while exploring different areas of the environmental field.
I am currently pursuing a Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management at UBC. Through this program, I have developed a strong interest in geomatics and remote sensing, particularly in how spatial data and satellite imagery can be used to analyze and monitor environmental change.
My academic and professional interests lie at the intersection of ecology and geospatial technology. I am particularly interested in how remote sensing and spatial analysis can be used to better understand ecological patterns, monitor landscapes, and support environmental decision-making.

title: Publications
text:
Peer-reviewed publications:
Despite listing them on your resume, it may also be pertinent to create a seperate tab for publications and reports. As your career progresses, this list may become quite long, so be sure to organize things. You may want to point to ‘most recent’ publications, or categorize things by project/topic.
